2) Differentiate the identity ln(E(t)) = t with respect to t. By the chain rule,
   (ln)'(E(t)) · E'(t) = 1.
   Using (ln)'(x) = 1/x gives
   (1/E(t)) · E'(t) = 1,
   hence E'(t) = E(t).

Equivalently, by the derivative-of-inverse formula,
E'(t) = 1 / (ln'(E(t))) = 1 / (1/E(t)) = E(t).
